text.title: SLO Definition Template Per Service Class Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: API SLO measures CPU < 80%; SLO 'green' but customers report 5xx.
antipattern.reason: Author copied a host-health metric as the SLI.
antipattern.note: Fix: Replace with HTTP success-rate SLI from the recording-rule template. CPU stays as an internal alert, not an SLO.

antipattern.description: Every SLO target is 99.9%; nobody can explain why; targets never recalibrate.
antipattern.reason: Targets copied from a Google blog post.
antipattern.note: Fix: For each SLO, write the rationale (customer SLA, peer benchmark, business cost of breach). Recalibrate yearly with the rationale.

antipattern.description: Ship-freeze fires on a 1h spike; release engineer paged at 3 AM for a transient.
antipattern.reason: Single-window alerting at 1h triggers freeze actions.
antipattern.note: Fix: Use multi-window multi-burn-rate. 1h window pages on-call (high-rate); 6h window triggers freeze (sustained).

antipattern.description: Three dashboards show three different availability numbers for the same service.
antipattern.reason: Each dashboard has its own PromQL; no recording rule.
antipattern.note: Fix: Author the SLI as a recording rule; dashboards and alerts reference the same series.
